sources.decision: Retain as the fast multilingual integration gate and expand to ten clips per language; do not use it as a podcast or audiobook proxy.

sources.dataset_version: MLS release published through OpenSLR SLR94; Hugging Face conversion revision pinned for acquisition
sources.decision: Use the acquired three-speaker development pilot for German, Spanish, French, and Portuguese. Audit the Spanish and Portuguese regional varieties rather than treating them as es-419 or pt-BR; references remain unverified dataset transcripts.

sources.dataset_version: LibriSpeech ASR corpus published through OpenSLR SLR12; Hugging Face conversion revision pinned for acquisition
sources.decision: Use three speaker- and chapter-stratified clean-validation excerpts as the English companion to the MLS development pilot because the selected Hugging Face MLS conversion does not expose its English partition.

sources.license: Apache-2.0 repository; underlying audio remains source-specific
sources.decision: Use professionally annotated evaluation excerpts for English after per-source rights review; never infer audio redistribution rights from the repository license.

sources.dataset_version: repository snapshot reviewed on the evidence date
sources.license: No explicit repository dataset, transcript, or audio license found
sources.decision: Retain as the strongest identified German podcast candidate because it supplies episode metadata and aligned text for professionally produced weekly podcasts. Do not run its downloader or admit any audio until the exact source item, transcript, and derived-clip rights are accepted.

sources.source_revision: RSS retrieved 2026-07-21T05:12:31Z; immutable original audio is digest-pinned per episode
sources.decision: Admit episodes 1, 2, and 4 as the first German held-out podcast source groups. The official feed licenses podcast episodes under CC BY 4.0, the selected originals are digest-pinned, and the published lightly smoothed transcripts remain draft material until an independent reviewer verifies repository-owned exact-audio gold transcripts.

sources.dataset_version: published IISYS full and clean archives; generator snapshot pinned separately
sources.license: Apache-2.0 generator repository; generated LibriVox audio and per-work text rights require separate review
sources.decision: Retain as the leading independent German audiobook candidate, with multiple speakers and clean 44.1 kHz archives. Do not infer dataset or audio rights from the generator's Apache-2.0 license; admit only work and speaker groups whose audio, text, and development-set isolation are accepted.

sources.dataset_version: Website and terms reviewed on the evidence date; terms dated October 2025
sources.license: Proprietary; written permission required for AI, automated analysis, research, development, testing, editing, and commercial use
sources.decision: Prioritize as a permission-only German audiobook candidate because it offers professionally presented recordings, named speakers, durations, and downloadable text for many works. Do not download, transcribe, normalize, crop, or analyze any file unless BUCHFUNK GmbH grants prior written permission covering the exact benchmark workflow.

sources.license: CC0 data; CC-BY-NC-4.0 code and pretrained models
sources.decision: Use only as a professional-speech robustness bridge. It is not a podcast substitute, and the upstream corpus has no transcribed Portuguese partition.

sources.license: per-source permission, permissive Creative Commons, public domain, or user-provided
sources.decision: Build a German-first, human-verified held-out set from at least three independent shows per language; keep audio local unless redistribution is explicitly licensed.

sources.license: per-source permission, permissive Creative Commons, public domain, or user-provided
sources.decision: Build a German-first, human-verified held-out set from at least three independent works and speakers per language. Exclude every FLEURS, MLS, and LibriSpeech development source group and keep audio local unless redistribution is explicitly licensed.
